article.title: LLD Help - Javascript
article.content:
Hey everyone,

I'm a backend developer with over 3 years of experience in Node.js (JavaScript). Given JavaScript’s loosely typed and functional nature, I haven't extensively used design/OOP principles in my journey. However, I have upcoming LLD interviews at Amazon and a few other companies, and after watching some LLD videos (mostly in Java), I'm feeling overwhelmed by the terminology and OOP-heavy approach.

While I can build large applications, I’m not well-versed in formal design principles. How should I approach LLD interviews, especially when coding in JavaScript? Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks!

post.content:
I have experience in Node.js and Python. I use Python for LLD. I would recommend you do the same.
After working with Typescript, I feel I can do it in typescript as well.

LLD is a language agnostic skills - language doesn't matter much.
TS > JS for OOPs
My open sourced repository to maintain OOPS and LLD interview codebase for Typescript. Feel free to contribute! \nhttps://github.com/1709abhishek/typescript-projects

Hi, in amazon mostly its a standard question and to be honest in JS you can code faster and do well in interview. use SOLID in your solution . 
Any State managment question will require different state classes and a main subject class using setState to switch b/w them whenever business logic requires it to change state. 
Any Stratergy Design will require an abstarct class being implemented in different strategies/ways and a processor using them as per the logic. 
Any Observers question will require a teacher/observer class attaching/detaching student classes with some common notify(punish) method exposed i.e student.punish('stand outisde class'). 
Any factory pattern will want you to create similar product through a factory class...like a write method from pencil and pen, but pencil write also having an option of erase OR perimum user, normal user in leetcode. this will be done by creating desired class using if else in a static factory method or by keeping a dummy/interface class WriterCreator acting as intermediator b/w client and concrete factory. that sums up to  WriterCreator(create) , CreatePen(create) returns pen object, CreatePencil(create) returns pencil object, Pen(write), Pencil(write,erase).
With this much implemented in JS, you can do anything everything.
